crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs:26:pub use gc::GcRef;
crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:60:pub use lua_types::gc::GcRef;

content.content:
217	// ── LuaTable ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
218	
219	/// A Lua table: hybrid array + hash map.
220	///
221	/// C: `struct Table` in `lobject.h`.  The full field mapping is in
222	/// `ANALYSES/types.tsv` under `Table → LuaTable`.
223	///
224	/// # Dummy hash part
225	///
226	/// When a table has no hash part, `self.node` is empty and
227	/// `self.lastfree` is `None`.  This replaces the C idiom of storing a
228	/// pointer to a shared static `dummynode_` and checking `lastfree == NULL`.
229	pub struct LuaTable {
230	    /// Bit-packed: lower bits = absent-metamethod flags; bit 7 = BIT_RAS.
231	    /// C: `lu_byte flags`.
232	    pub flags: TableFlags,
233	    /// `log2` of the hash-part node count.  `1u32 << lsizenode` = capacity.
234	    /// C: `lu_byte lsizenode`.
235	    pub lsizenode: u8,
236	    /// Array-part size hint or real size depending on `flags.is_real_asize()`.
237	    /// C: `unsigned int alimit`.
238	    pub alimit: u32,
239	    /// The array part; 0-based in Rust (Lua index `k` → `self.array[k-1]`).
240	    /// C: `TValue *array`.
241	    pub array: Vec<LuaValue>,
242	    /// The hash part; length is `1 << lsizenode` when not dummy, 0 when dummy.
243	    /// C: `Node *node`.
244	    pub node: Vec<TableNode>,
245	    /// Cursor into `self.node` for free-slot scanning (one-past the last tried
246	    /// position).  `None` when using the dummy hash part.
247	    /// C: `Node *lastfree` (NULL = dummy).
248	    pub lastfree: Option<usize>,
249	    /// Optional metatable.
250	    /// C: `struct Table *metatable`.
251	    pub metatable: Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>,
252	}
253	
254	impl LuaTable {
255	    // ── Predicate helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
256	
257	    /// `isdummy(t)` — true when the table has no allocated hash part.
258	    #[inline]
259	    pub fn is_dummy(&self) -> bool {
260	        self.lastfree.is_none()
261	    }
262	
263	    /// `sizenode(t)` — nominal hash-part capacity (`1 << lsizenode`).
264	    /// This is the modulo used for hash indexing, not the vec length.
265	    #[inline]
266	    fn sizenode(&self) -> u32 {
267	        1u32 << self.lsizenode
268	    }
269	
270	    /// `allocsizenode(t)` — 0 when dummy, else `1 << lsizenode`.
271	    #[inline]
272	    pub fn alloc_sizenode(&self) -> u32 {
273	        if self.is_dummy() { 0 } else { self.sizenode() }
274	    }
275	
276	    /// `isrealasize(t)` accessor; delegates to `TableFlags`.
277	    #[inline]
278	    fn is_real_asize(&self) -> bool { self.flags.is_real_asize() }
279	
280	    // ── ispow2 (C macro with 0-as-pow2 semantics) ─────────────────────────
281	
282	    /// `ispow2(x)` — C treats 0 as a power of two (`(x & (x-1)) == 0`).
283	    #[inline]
284	    fn is_pow2(x: u32) -> bool {
285	        x == 0 || x.is_power_of_two()
286	    }
287	
288	    // ── luaH_realasize ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
289	
290	    /// Returns the real size of the array part.
291	    ///
292	    /// When `flags.is_real_asize()`, this is just `alimit`.  Otherwise
293	    /// `alimit` is a hint and the real size is the next power of two above it.

295	    /// C: `unsigned int luaH_realasize (const Table *t)` (`LUAI_FUNC`).

content.text:
Build is green.

**Report**

- **File modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1213`
- **What `registry_get` does now:** Clones the registry value from `GlobalState.l_registry`; if it's a `LuaValue::Table`, performs an integer-keyed lookup via `LuaTableRefExt::get(&LuaValue::Int(key as i64))`, returning `Nil` for any other variant. This mirrors the C macro `getGtable(L) → array[key-1]` and the general `lua_rawgeti(L, LUA_REGISTRYINDEX, key)` shape, dispatched through the established `LuaTableRefExt` adapter so it stays consistent with how every other `LuaValue::Table` access works in this crate (`lua_types::value::LuaTable` placeholder; rich `lua_vm::table::LuaTable` wire-up is the future reconcile step).
- **Surfacing `todo!()`s inside the new impl:** None — `LuaTableRefExt::get` is the no-op-returns-`Nil` Phase-A implementation, not a `todo!()`. The next runtime panic will come from whatever caller acts on the returned `Nil` (likely `aux_get_str` calling `table_get_with_tm` or downstream `set_field` paths).
- **`cargo build -p lua-cli`:** green (warnings only).

result:
Build is green.

**Report**

- **File modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1213`
- **What `registry_get` does now:** Clones the registry value from `GlobalState.l_registry`; if it's a `LuaValue::Table`, performs an integer-keyed lookup via `LuaTableRefExt::get(&LuaValue::Int(key as i64))`, returning `Nil` for any other variant. This mirrors the C macro `getGtable(L) → array[key-1]` and the general `lua_rawgeti(L, LUA_REGISTRYINDEX, key)` shape, dispatched through the established `LuaTableRefExt` adapter so it stays consistent with how every other `LuaValue::Table` access works in this crate (`lua_types::value::LuaTable` placeholder; rich `lua_vm::table::LuaTable` wire-up is the future reconcile step).
- **Surfacing `todo!()`s inside the new impl:** None — `LuaTableRefExt::get` is the no-op-returns-`Nil` Phase-A implementation, not a `todo!()`. The next runtime panic will come from whatever caller acts on the returned `Nil` (likely `aux_get_str` calling `table_get_with_tm` or downstream `set_field` paths).
- **`cargo build -p lua-cli`:** green (warnings only).
